pysr_finding_note: PySR 1.5.5 docstring lists X as 'ndarray | pandas.DataFrame' and _validate_and_set_fit_params explicitly does 'isinstance(X, pd.DataFrame)' for the space-rename safety branch -- the branch is SKIPPED on polars input. sklearn validate_data does accept polars in practice (>=1.3 dataframe-protocol support), so polars technically reaches Julia and feature_names_in_ propagates, but the contract is undocumented and PySR's pandas-only sanitisation is silently bypassed. Decision: keep the to_pandas() boundary after polars-side fillna; the existing bench-attempt-rejected comment already shows use_pyarrow_extension_array=True is 3x SLOWER on the PySR+sklearn check_array path. Polars-native fit is NOT future-proof for this dependency.

A2#6.notes: Moved median imputation entirely to the polars side via cs.numeric().fill_nan/fill_null so the downstream to_pandas() allocates exactly once and no pd.DataFrame.fillna broadcast-copy fires on the polars branch. Added MLFRAME_PYSR_INPUT_BYTES_LIMIT byte-size gate (default 2 GB) on both pandas and polars branches: refuses runs that would peak ~2x because PySR densifies through sklearn check_array AND the Julia bridge.

A2#11.notes: Replaced the in-place tmp_df.columns = _final_names assignment + cat-cast loop pattern with a dict-of-Series fresh-frame construction (pd.DataFrame({new: tmp_df[old], ...}, copy=False)). Builds a brand-new BlockManager so the renamed frame is no longer aliased to the caller's view (pandas df.sample() shared the column-Index object AND for view inputs the BlockManager could be aliased). ndarrays themselves stay zero-copy.
